articles.title: Weather Today [23 March 2026]: Check IMD Forecast, Snow & Rain Predictions for Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu Kashmir, Ahmedabad, Punjab, Hyderabad, Uttar Pradesh - The Sunday Guardian
articles.full_text:
Weather Today (23 March 2026): As India progresses further into the summer season, a weak Western Disturbance is keeping weather activity alive in the northern regions. While the massive 1,000-km rain band from earlier days has subsided, isolated precipitation and a significant rise in temperatures are the key features for today. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasts a mix of rising heat across the plains and light snow in the hills, signaling a definitive shift toward the summer season.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), maximum temperatures are expected to rise by 4–7°C over Northwest, Central, and East India over the next few days. A weak Western Disturbance, however, continues to influence the Western Himalayas and parts of the northern plains, bringing isolated weather activity.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?
A weak Western Disturbance is affecting the Western Himalayas, bringing isolated light rain and snowfall to the higher reaches. Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand are likely to witness scattered precipitation today. Higher altitudes may experience temporary road disruptions due to fresh snowfall. The lower hills and plains of these regions will remain largely dry with partly cloudy skies.
Why is There Weather Change Across Parts of the Country?
The weather pattern on 23 March 2026 is being shaped by several meteorological factors:
- Rising Temperatures: A gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 4–7°C is expected over Northwest, Central, and East India as high-pressure systems build, marking the onset of early summer heat.
- Remnant Western Disturbance: A weak Western Disturbance is lingering over the Western Himalayas, leading to isolated snow and rain in the high-altitude areas.
- Isolated Thunderstorm Activity: An induced cyclonic circulation is leading to isolated thunderstorms and gusty winds (30-40 kmph) in parts of East Rajasthan and Madhya Maharashtra.
- Seasonal Transition: The country is moving from a weak La Niña phase toward ENSO-neutral conditions, which can lead to variable weather patterns during the March–April transition, with sudden spikes in temperature.
Delhi NCR Weather Today (Delhi, Noida, Gurgaon, Ghaziabad)
Minimum: 15°C to 17°C | Maximum: 31°C to 33°C
The national capital region will experience generally cloudy skies with a possibility of very light rain or drizzle, accompanied by thunderstorms or lightning during the forenoon to afternoon. Light winds from the northwest are expected during the day. The Air Quality Index (AQI) is likely to remain in the “Moderate” range.
Mumbai Weather Today
Minimum: ~24°C | Maximum: ~36°C to 37°C
Mumbai will witness hot and humid conditions with moderate sea winds expected throughout the day. The IMD has indicated that “hot and humid” conditions will persist in the city and its suburbs, with the high moisture content in the air making conditions feel quite warm, especially during the afternoon.
Chennai Weather Today
Minimum: 25°C to 26°C | Maximum: 37°C to 38°C
Chennai is set for a largely warm and humid day with no major rainfall expected. Despite the sea breeze offering slight moderation, the combination of warmth and humidity will make conditions feel quite uncomfortable, especially during the afternoon.
Karnataka Weather Today
Minimum: 18°C to 22°C | Maximum: 31°C to 35°C
Karnataka will see generally pleasant mornings with warmer afternoons. The state is mostly dry, but isolated light showers are possible in the coastal belts. Interior Karnataka may experience clear skies and a gradual rise in daytime temperatures.
Rajasthan Weather Today
Minimum: 21°C to 24°C | Maximum: 38°C to 41°C
Rajasthan is experiencing clear skies and rising daytime heat, with dry weather prevailing. Isolated thunderstorms and gusty winds (30-40 kmph) are likely in parts of East Rajasthan. Hot winds are likely to prevail during the daytime.
Uttar Pradesh Weather Today
Minimum: 18°C to 22°C | Maximum: 35°C to 38°C
Dry heat is intensifying across Uttar Pradesh, marking early summer conditions. The state will experience partly cloudy skies with some morning fog in isolated pockets. The maximum temperature can reach up to 38°C in cities like Kanpur and Lucknow.
Punjab Weather Today
Minimum: 15°C to 17°C | Maximum: 32°C to 34°C
Punjab will remain partly cloudy to sunny with mostly stable weather. Daytime temperatures are expected to be slightly above normal, with cooler evenings offering some relief.
Hyderabad Weather Today
Minimum: ~22°C to 23°C | Maximum: ~34°C to 35°C
Hyderabad will see dry heat continue with clear skies. The afternoons are expected to be particularly hot, though the nights may offer slight relief. No significant rainfall is anticipated in the city today.
Ahmedabad Weather Today
Minimum: 22°C to 24°C | Maximum: 38°C to 39°C
Ahmedabad and the wider Gujarat region are bracing for hot and dry conditions with a steady rise in daytime heat. According to the IMD, temperatures are expected to approach heatwave levels in isolated pockets.
Jammu and Kashmir Weather Today
Minimum: 5°C to 9°C (Valley) | Maximum: 15°C to 18°C
Light rain or snowfall continues in the higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir, accompanied by strong winds and cold conditions. The valley of Srinagar will see cold mornings, and the fresh Western Disturbance may lead to temporary disruptions in high-altitude areas due to snowfall.
IMD Safety Advisory
Due to the possibility of isolated thunderstorms and gusty winds in some states, and rising heat in others, the IMD suggests:
- Stay hydrated and avoid peak afternoon sun, especially in heatwave-affected areas.
- If thunderstorms occur, stay indoors, close windows, and avoid taking shelter under trees.
- Travelers in the Western Himalayas should be prepared for potential road disruptions due to snowfall.
City-Wise Sunset & Sunrise Timing Today (23 March 2026)
On 23 March 2026, sunrise across major Indian cities occurs between 6:12 AM and 6:43 AM, while sunset ranges from 6:22 PM to 6:52 PM. Most cities are experiencing roughly 12 hours and 10-12 minutes of daylight as we move past the spring equinox.
Key Observations
- Day Length: Most cities are experiencing roughly 12 hours and 10-12 minutes of daylight as we move past the spring equinox.
- Golden Hour: For photographers, the morning golden hour in major metros like Delhi ends around 6:55 AM, and the evening golden hour typically begins near 6:00 PM.
- Civil Twilight: In most regions, civil twilight lasts for approximately 21–24 minutes after sunset, after which artificial lighting is generally required for outdoor activities.
FAQs: Weather Today Update
Q. Are there any travel or safety warnings?
A. Travelers in the Western Himalayas, especially in high-altitude areas of J&K, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand, should be prepared for potential road disruptions due to fresh snowfall. In the plains, authorities advise staying hydrated and avoiding peak afternoon sun, especially in regions where temperatures are rising.
Q. Will temperatures rise across the country?
A. Yes, maximum temperatures are expected to rise by 4–7°C over Northwest, Central, and East India over the next few days, signaling a definitive shift towards the summer season.
Q. Will there be any rain today?
A. Yes, isolated rain and thunderstorms are possible in parts of East Rajasthan, Madhya Maharashtra, and Northeast India. Light rain or drizzle is also possible in Delhi NCR during the forenoon to afternoon. Widespread rainfall is not expected in most plains.
Q. Where will it snow today?
A. Light snowfall is possible in the higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand.
Q. Is a heatwave expected soon?
A. While a full-scale heatwave is not yet widespread, the IMD has noted that temperatures are rising toward heatwave levels in isolated pockets of Gujarat and Rajasthan. Other parts of western and central India are also approaching heatwave thresholds.
Disclaimer: Weather forecasts are for guidance only. Conditions may change rapidly. Always check local authorities for emergencies and travel alerts.
